channel_2_marmot_event_bus.own_echo_gate: NONE — but ChatStoreContext comment claiming 'own-send echo path' is INCORRECT (see Q1 answer below)

existing_handler_modules.chatPersistence.ts: CHAT_MESSAGE_KIND=9, appendMessage, loadMessages — IDB write module
existing_handler_modules.groupStorage.ts: mergeMemberScore, mergeMemberProfile, updateMemberScoreNickname — IDB write module

reusable_pubsub_patterns.module_singleton_store: app/src/lib/unreadStore.ts — let state + Set<listener> + emit() + useSyncExternalStore

similar_features.retrospective_flag: Both channels fire only for peer messages. The ChatStoreContext 'own-send echo path' comment is incorrect per Q1 finding. Dual-dispatch applies to all inbound rumors, not only reactions.

architecture.paradigm: Functional core + imperative shell, with package-by-feature in app/src/lib/marmot/

Q1_own_send_emit_timing.answer: marmot-ts does NOT emit 'applicationMessage' for own-send rumors
Q1_own_send_emit_timing.evidence: sendApplicationRumor adds event id to #sentEventIds before publish. When relay echo arrives and ingest() processes it, the id is found in #sentEventIds, deleted, and the event is yielded as {kind:'skipped', reason:'self-echo'} — emit('applicationMessage') at marmot-group.js:840 is never reached.
Q1_own_send_emit_timing.implication: ChatStoreContext.tsx:254 comment 'marmot-ts re-delivers our own rumors back through this event (own-send echo path)' is INCORRECT. Both channels deliver ONLY peer inbound messages. The ChatStoreContext kind-7 branch is a second inbound handler, not an own-echo handler. Dual-dispatch applies to all kinds, not just kind-7.

architecture.Q1_implication_for_design: The dispatcher's own-echo section (spec §Own-send echo handling, steps 3-5) describes behavior that does NOT currently occur via the bus — the optimistic write in sendMessage IS the only write for own messages. The IDB idempotence step (step 4) is a no-op defense, which is fine to keep, but the chatVersion bump in step 5 will NOT fire for own-sends via the bus. This is the current behavior too — ChatStoreContext uses local setState for own-send display, not chatVersion. Keep as-is: the dispatcher only processes peer messages; own-send display is handled optimistically.

context_dependency_graph.version_counter_as_idb_invalidation: setChatVersion(v+1) in handler → ChatStoreContext useEffect re-reads IDB messages

idb_function_signatures.mergeMemberProfile: async function mergeMemberProfile(groupId: string, profile: MemberProfile): Promise<boolean> — groupStorage.ts:140
idb_function_signatures.updateMemberScoreNickname: async function updateMemberScoreNickname(groupId: string, pubkeyHex: string, nickname: string): Promise<void> — groupStorage.ts:174
idb_function_signatures.getPoll: async function getPoll(groupId: string, pollId: string): Promise<Poll | null> — pollPersistence.ts:96
idb_function_signatures.applyInboundRumor: async function applyInboundRumor(thread: ReactionThreadKey, rumor: {...}): Promise<{messageId: string} | null> — reactions/api.ts:289

kind_constants_locations.kind_7_reaction: NO named constant — both MarmotContext.tsx:781 and ChatStoreContext.tsx:251 use literal 7. REACTION_RUMOR_KIND=7 should be introduced in reactionHandler.ts.
kind_constants_locations.CHAT_MESSAGE_KIND_note: chatPersistence.ts:12 re-exports from or is co-located with kind constant; chatHandler.ts will import from chatPersistence.ts

special_closures: sendRumor: (group, content) => Promise<void> — for profileRequestHandler self-reply; injected at subscribe() time from MarmotContext which has mlsGroup in scope

existing_lru_patterns.NDK_event_level_dedup: app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts:207 — Set<string> of Nostr event IDs, NOT ApplicationRumor.id level
existing_lru_patterns.application_rumor_lru: DOES NOT EXIST — must be built new in applicationRumorDispatcher.ts

architecture.retrospective_flag: Q1 resolved: marmot-ts #sentEventIds drops own-send echoes before applicationMessage fires. ChatStoreContext comment is incorrect. The spec's Own-send echo section describes a no-op for own-sends via the bus — the optimistic write in sendMessage is the only write, which is already the correct current behavior.

testing_and_conventions.unit_test_run_command: make test-unit → cd app && npx vitest run
testing_and_conventions.unit_test_marmot_dir: app/tests/unit/marmot/ — exists with 15 files, no dispatcher test yet

idb_mock_strategy.default: vi.mock('idb-keyval', ...) with module-level Map — used in 13 of 14 marmot unit tests
idb_mock_strategy.integration: import 'fake-indexeddb/auto' — only for modules using idb-keyval createStore (profileRequestStorage). Not needed for appendMessage, savePoll, applyInboundRumor since they use the default store.

testing_and_conventions.canonical_unit_test_model: app/tests/unit/marmot/groupReactions.test.ts — Map-mock idb-keyval + dynamic import after vi.mock + describe/it/expect/vi/beforeEach
testing_and_conventions.e2e_runner: Playwright, chromium only, fullyParallel: false, workers: 1
testing_and_conventions.multi_tab_pattern: Two separate BrowserContext instances via browser.newContext(), each with localStorage identity injection via addInitScript

idb_helpers_e2e.clearAppState_note: clearAppState deletes 9 MDK-named IDB databases but NOT the keyval-store used by reactions/chat/unread — tests verifying IDB write counts must use readIdbRecord against 'keyval-store'/'keyval'

window_nostlingTest_surface.ar6_idb_write_counter: Does NOT exist. Must be added. Options: (a) extend __nostlingTest.onChatIdbWrite hooked into chatPersistence.appendMessage, or (b) use readIdbRecord after dispatch to count IDB rows.

test_conventions.ac_references_in_descriptions: Every test description references AC numbers (AC-38, AC-40, etc.)
test_conventions.unit_describe_style: import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'

groups_direct_chat_no_duplicates_spec.coverage: DM ContactChat path only, not MLS GroupChat. Groups duplicate-dispatch isolation (AR-21) is NOT currently covered.
groups_direct_chat_no_duplicates_spec.pattern_to_reuse: WebSocket.prototype.send monkey-patch to force race conditions — adaptable for groups-dispatch-isolation.spec.ts
